title: Cosmo's Number Parade
description: Cosmo the Labrador counts his friends in a Dr. Seuss–style rhyme. Learn 1–10 with rhythm, rhyme, and a lot of tail-wagging.
summary_pt_br: Cosmo, o labrador, conta seus amigos numa rima estilo Dr. Seuss. Aprenda de 1 a 10 com ritmo, rima e muito abanar de rabo.

scenes.subtitle_en: A counting song in the classroom
scenes.subtitle_pt: Uma canção de contagem na sala de aula

scenes.text_en:
Cosmo the dog had a lot to say.
He couldn't speak English — but he WAGGED all day.
Mr. Kumar said, 'Class, today we'll count.
Cosmo will help. He's a counting hound.'
scenes.text_pt:
Cosmo, o cachorro, tinha muito a dizer.
Não falava inglês — só sabia abanar.
Mr. Kumar falou: 'Turma, hoje a gente vai contar.
O Cosmo vai ajudar — um cão de contar.'

scenes.text_en:
ONE little Cosmo, wagging with glee.
TWO shiny eyes, as brown as can be.
THREE orange cats snoozing on the floor.
FOUR funny feet that tap at the door.
FIVE little friends who laugh more and more.
scenes.text_pt:
UM Cosminho, abanando feliz.
DOIS olhos marrons, que nem raiz.
TRÊS gatos laranjas no chão a dormir.
QUATRO patinhas batendo a abrir.
CINCO amiguinhos rindo a sorrir.

exercise.question: How many orange cats are in the rhyme?
exercise.explanation: 'THREE orange cats snoozing on the floor.' The rhyme uses 3.

scenes.text_en:
SIX silly birds in the Praia do Sol sky.
SEVEN sharp pencils that Maya holds high.
EIGHT hungry surfers in Rafa's food line.
NINE noisy waves — every wave is a sign.
TEN thumping tails. Yes — ten tails! All MINE!
scenes.text_pt:
SEIS passarinhos no céu da Praia do Sol.
SETE lápis apontados nas mãos da Maya, pode crer.
OITO surfistas famintos na fila do Rafa.
NOVE ondas barulhentas — cada uma diz algo a saber.
DEZ rabos batendo. Isso mesmo — dez rabos MEUS!

scenes.title: How to count things
scenes.body_en: English puts the number BEFORE the noun, just like Portuguese: 'three cats', 'ten fingers'. For plurals of regular nouns, add -s: cat → cats, bird → birds. Some are irregular: mouse → mice, child → children. But that's a story for another day.
scenes.body_pt: Em inglês, o número vem ANTES do substantivo: 'three cats' (três gatos). O plural regular é só adicionar -s. Irregulares (mouse → mice, child → children) ficam para depois.

exercise.question: Cosmo wags his ___ tails? (He only has one!)
exercise.explanation: Cosmo is ONE dog with ONE tail — the 'ten tails' was just a silly brag. ONE is the number 1 in English.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Cosmo tem UM rabo só. 'Ten tails' foi só exagero.

exercise.explanation: Numbers 6–10, matched to their English names. EIGHT has a silent 'gh' — pronounced /eɪt/, just 'ate'.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Eight se lê /eit/ — igual a 'ate' (o verbo comer no passado).

exercise.question: Bia asks: 'How many surfboards do you have?' She has 2. What does she say?
exercise.explanation: Plural -s on the noun ('surfboards'). Subject-verb: I HAVE, not 'I has' (that's he/she/it).
exercise.hint_pt_br: 'I have' + substantivo no plural. 'Has' é só para he/she/it.

scenes.text_en:
Mr. Kumar grinned and clapped once or twice.
'Counting with Cosmo is always quite nice.
Tomorrow,' he said, 'we'll count even more —
twenty, and thirty, and right up to a hundred — or four!'
scenes.text_pt:
Mr. Kumar sorriu e bateu palmas ali.
'Contar com o Cosmo é bom de seguir.
Amanhã,' ele disse, 'vamos mais além —
vinte, e trinta, e até cem, meu bem!'

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Cosmo's Number Parade". Cosmo the Labrador counts his friends in a Dr. Seuss–style rhyme. Learn 1–10 with rhythm, rhyme, and a lot of tail-wagging. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Cosmo's Number Parade". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'Cosmo's Number Parade'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "Cosmo's Number Parade".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.

sources.label: VOA Learning English
sources.pt_hint: Notícias em inglês lento — ótimo para ouvido iniciante.
